We used to use a legacy system, but nowadays we automate everything.
The team used to update the spreadsheet by hand, but gradually we switched.
The new platform replaced the manual routine completely.
We used to work with the old legacy system.
The team used to update the prices by hand.
I did not use to trust the data.
Did you use to work on weekends?
We used to update prices by hand, but nowadays the system automates it.
Daniela, do you remember how we used to work before the migration?
Of course. We used to work with the old legacy system, and it used to crash every week.
And the prices? Did you use to update them by hand?
Yes. The team used to update everything on a huge spreadsheet, store by store.
So how did everything change?
Gradually, we started to automate the work, and a new platform replaced the spreadsheets.
I did not use to trust the new tools at first, honestly.
Me neither. But nowadays the whole routine is faster, and we never go back to the old way.
The team used to update prices on a spreadsheet.
Nowadays we automate everything, gradually.

Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to one short voice note about used to, the structure for past habits and routines. Use it for something that was true before but is not true now. I used to work with the old legacy system every day. We used to do everything by hand, with paper and spreadsheets. The store used to close at six, and the team used to count the stock manually. Now, here is the key. The form is always used to plus the base verb. Say I used to work, never I used to working, and never I use to work in the past. For questions and negatives, the d disappears. Did you use to work late? No, I did not use to work on weekends. Remember, used to talks about a habit that has changed, so it always points to a contrast with nowadays. Try it. Think about your old routine at work and how it is different now. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus. I am leaving you a voicemail about the system migration project. So, I was thinking about how much our routine has changed. Two years ago, we used to work with the old legacy system, and it used to crash almost every week. The team used to update the prices by hand, store by store, on a huge spreadsheet. We used to spend three full days on the monthly report. We did not use to trust the data, honestly. Then, gradually, we started to automate everything. Now the new platform does it in minutes, and nobody uses paper anymore. Funny, right? We used to complain about the old way, but we also used to know every step by heart. Nowadays it is faster, but I think we should still document the manual process for new people. Call me back when you can. Talk soon, Daniela.
